revision.text:
[[ja:安倉高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Akura Takatsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Akura Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Remaining Kofun | alternate_name = Torishima Kofun{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|qid=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.79658333333333|N|135.37358333333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 17m
Height 2.7m | burial = {{ill|Vertical stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Contains {{ill|Split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}}?) | burial goods = Bronze mirrors, jade ornaments, iron objects | built = Late 4th century | Interred_individual = Unknown | designation1 = Takarazuka City Designated Historic site "Akura Takatsuka Kofun" | designation1 = burial goods (Hyōgo Prefecture Designated designation1) | notes = Excavation of a Red Crow Seventh Year Inscription Mirror | map = Japan Hyōgo | type = Circular Kofun | map_dot_label = Akura Takatsuka Kofun | map_dot_label = left | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Akura Takatsuka Kofun''' (安倉高塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Akura Minami|ja|安倉南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Minami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安倉 (宝塚市)|1=Akura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q115176514}}, {{ill|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|en|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|qid=Q823967|lt=Takarazuka|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a circular tumulus and has been Historic site as a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} by Takarazuka City. burial goods excavated from the site have been Historic site as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} by Hyōgo Prefecture. == Overview == The kofun is located on the western edge of the {{ill|Itami Plateau|ja|伊丹台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itami Plateau|1=Itami Plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21652450}}, on the left bank of the {{ill|Muko River|en|Muko River|qid=Q6933569|lt=Muko River|1=Muko River}} in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. It is believed that a group of tombs known as the "Akura Seven Tombs" once existed nearby, but only this kofun remains today{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. The kofun was largely destroyed in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Shōwa 12), during which the stone chamber and burial burial goods were discovered{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. The tumulus was originally 17 meters in diameter and 2.7 meters in height, but today only the northern half remains{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. The burial facility is a vertical stone chamber at the summit, with an estimated length of 6.3 meters (only one-fifth remains), a width of 0.75 meters, and a depth of 0.6 meters{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. The chamber was built using river stones, with stone slabs covering the top{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. Clay floors inside the chamber suggest the use of a split bamboo-shaped wooden coffin{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. burial goods found include two bronze mirrors, beads, and iron tools. One of the mirrors bears an inscription reading "Red Crow Seventh Year" (244 CE), making it a significant discovery. This kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 4th century{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}Historical site intypeation board.. The mirror's inscription closely aligns with the period when {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|qid=Q234451|lt=Himiko|1=Himiko}} is said to have sent an envoy to {{ill|Cao Wei|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cao Wei|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124257886}}, making it an important find in the study of early Japan-China relations. The kofun was Historic site a {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} by Takarazuka City in {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Shōwa 45) and its excavated burial goods were Historic site as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} by Hyōgo Prefecture in {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51). == Excavation History == * {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Shōwa 12): Southern half of the tumulus destroyed; discovery of the stone chamber and burial goods{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. * {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} (Shōwa 45), November 3: Designated as a Takarazuka City Historic site. * {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), March 23: excavations Historic site as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Hyōgo Prefecture. == burial goods == [[File:安倉高塚古墳出土 赤烏七年画文帯対置式神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Red Crow Seventh Year Inscription Mirror (Replica)|qq=}}{{small|The original is a Hyōgo Prefecture Designated designation1, displayed at the Hyōgo Prefectural Archaeological Museum.|qq=}}]] excavations include two bronze mirrors: one {{ill|Shinju-kyo|en|Shinju-kyo|qid=Q7497614|lt=Shinju Mirror|1=Shinju-kyo}} and one [[:en:Flower mirror|Naikōka Pattern Mirror]]. The Shinju Mirror features a four-god, four-beast motif with a semicircular rectangular band, measuring 17.5 cm in diameter[https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/kanko/0000001852.html Akura Takatsuka Kofun burial goods] (Himeji City website).. The "Red Crow" inscription refers to the {{ill|吳|en|吳|qid=Q96621022|lt=Eastern Wu|1=吳}} dynasty of China, specifically the year 244 CE. Among Japanese finds, only one other mirror bears a Wu-era inscription, from the {{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}} in Yamanashi Prefecture, making this an important artifact in studying early international relations{{Sfn|Akura Takatsuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1999|qq=}}. The Naikōka Pattern Mirror is a domestically made imitation. Other burial goods include jade ornaments and iron tools. These burial goods are now housed at the {{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Museum of History|en|Hyōgo Prefectural Museum of History|qid=Q11393043|lt=Hyōgo Prefectural Museum of History|1=Hyōgo Prefectural Museum of History}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}. == Historic site == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designated Historic site === * Important designation1 (Tangible designation1) ** excavations from Akura Takatsuka Kofun - Details: *** Bronze Semi-Circular Shinju Mirror (1 piece) - Inscribed "Red Crow Seventh Year" (Nationally Important Art Object) *** Naikōka Pattern Mirror (1 piece) *** Three tube-shaped jade beads, two small jade beads *** (Additional) Sword blades, adze, hoe tips, iron spears (9 fragments in total) === Takarazuka City Designated Historic site === * Historic site ** Akura Takatsuka Kofun - Designated November 3, 1970. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/kanko/0000001852.html Akura Takatsuka Kofun burial goods] - Himeji City website * {{translated page|ja|安倉高塚古墳|version=99995236|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:安倉高塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q83070704}} [[Category:Kofun in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal designated historic sites in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:安倉 (宝塚市)]] [[Category:History of Takarazuka, Hyogo]]
